slides.title: What is cURL?
slides.content:
# Client URL Library

cURL allows you to make HTTP requests to other servers from PHP. It's great for consuming APIs, fetching web pages, or automating tasks.

Basic steps:
1. Initialize a cURL session with `curl_init()`.
2. Set options with `curl_setopt()`.
3. Execute with `curl_exec()`.
4. Close with `curl_close()`.

slides.title: Simple GET Request
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, 'https://api.example.com/data');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
$response = curl_exec($ch);
if (curl_error($ch)) {
    echo 'cURL error: ' . curl_error($ch);
} else {
    echo $response;
}
curl_close($ch);
?>

slides.title: POST Request and Headers
POST JSON Data
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$data = ['name' => 'Jane', 'email' => 'jane@example.com'];
$jsonData = json_encode($data);
$ch = curl_init('https://api.example.com/users');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $jsonData);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ['Content-Type: application/json']);
$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
?>

quizData.question: Which cURL option returns the response as a string instead of outputting it?
quizData.explanation: CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER makes curl_exec return the response as a string.
